verdict_rationale: All 4 BLOCKING v3.0.1 gates PASS (canonical, prefers-reduced-motion N/A, CLS hygiene, semantic ≥80). No critical / serious axe violations. JSON-LD parses cleanly on all 8 indexed pages. Live site reachable. Two ship-blockers gated under 'warnings' because they are NOT v3.0.1 gates but are pre-launch must-fix: (1) contact-form Formspree action is 'PLACEHOLDER_FORM_ID' — every submission will 404; (2) pricing.js renders tier-card CTAs with bare-absolute href='/contact.html#contact-form' which resolves to derrick-pixel.github.io/contact.html (broken — needs './contact.html#contact-form' or 'contact.html#contact-form'). Six secondary findings (heading-skip site-wide, --muted2 / --accent2 contrast borderline, html lang='en' not 'en-SG', 404 thin content, admin-page-stamp missing, FAQ accordion not bound to JSON-LD source-of-truth). None block the v3.0.1 gates. Fix the two ship-blockers, then ship.

prefers_reduced_motion.note: No production page ships scroll-reveal, count-up, or canvas animation. CSS transitions are state-driven (hover, focus, expand) — out of scope per handbook's animation list. colors.html is the only file with transitions and is internal/transient (sitemap.json should mark internal:true; flagged below).

cls_hygiene.note: Every has explicit width + height. Logos serve at width=120 height=32/36 in nav and footer. No uses loading='lazy' or loading='eager' explicitly, but since logos are above-fold and small (<2KB each), this is acceptable (loading attribute is best-practice but not a v3.0.1 gate).

semantic_audit_avg_score.note: Floor 80 met by wide margin. Single recurring medium violation across all pages: seo-heading-skip — footer column h4 follows last main h2 with no h3 between (see qa-report.json.semantic_audit[]).

violations.evidence: Last main <h2> ('Fifteen minutes, one screen-share, no deck.') is followed by footer <h4 class="site-footer__col-heading"> with no <h3> in between.
h2→h4 jump at footer (same site-wide pattern).
h2→h4 jump at footer (same site-wide pattern). Note: tier-card h3 names are rendered by pricing.js client-side, so first paint of the noscript view skips h2→h4 at the tier-grid host too.
h2→h4 jump at footer (same site-wide pattern).
h2→h4 jump at footer (same site-wide pattern).
h2→h4 jump at footer (same site-wide pattern).
h2→h4 jump at footer. Top-5 cards, competitor table rows, and radar legend are rendered client-side from JSON; h3 nodes are injected by admin.js after first paint.
h2→h4 jump at footer. Personas / tiers / attack-plans render client-side from admin-insights.js.
Main word count = 62 (handbook floor = 100; ≥300 ideal for content pages). 404 pages are conventionally terse — flagged but accepted.

prefers_reduced_motion.note: No scroll-reveal, count-up, or canvas animation on the page. CSS only ships state-driven transitions (hover/focus) — not in the handbook's animation list. assets/js/nav.js uses scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth'}) for in-page anchors; modern Chrome/Safari natively respect prefers-reduced-motion for smooth-scroll.

cls_hygiene.note: 2 tags (logo in header + footer), both have width=120 height=32/36. No without dimensions site-wide. No loading attribute — acceptable for logos <3KB above-fold.

axe_violations.selector: var(--muted2) on var(--bg)  —  e.g. .tier-card__what-heading--out, .feature-card__body (small contexts at .compute pages)
axe_violations.fix_suggestion: Contrast(--muted2 #6b7785, --bg #fafafa) = 4.37 — fails AA for normal body text (≥4.5 needed). Either darken --muted2 to #6a7480 (4.51) or restrict its usage to large text (≥18.66px or ≥14px bold). Currently used on small UI strings ('What is out', tier card persona caption). Same finding for --accent2 #F26522 used as color: on small UI strings (3.02 contrast).

axe_violations.selector: footer h4.site-footer__col-heading following main's last h2
axe_violations.fix_suggestion:
Either promote footer column headings from

to

, or insert a hidden

Site footer

as a contentinfo landmark heading. Site-wide pattern across all 9 pages.

iphone_13.notes: Responsive breakpoints @640px / 768px / 920px / 1024px all defined in assets/css. Hamburger (.nav-toggle) shows below 920px and hides above (via @media min-width:920px). Mobile menu (.mobile-nav) is full-width drawer with link list; assets/js/nav.js binds toggle to aria-expanded and closes menu on link click — verified at nav.js:17-22. Hero stack (.hero__visual--grid) becomes single-column below 1024px. FAQ accordion .accordion-item toggles via JS — verified at nav.js:38-46.

pixel_7.notes: Same responsive behaviour as iPhone 13. Tap targets: .nav-toggle is 28x28px (the spans inside are 20x2px) — handbook recommends ≥44px. Header CTA .btn--primary has min padding 12px×24px which yields ~44px tap area. Mobile nav links are full-width — ample tap area. .accordion-header is full-width button — ample tap area.

ipad.notes: At 768px, .pricing-grid switches to 2-col (default 1-col below 768), .feature-grid--3 switches to 2-col, and hamburger still shows (because hamburger hides only at ≥920px). Above 1024px, .pricing-grid still 2-col until 1100px (the 5-col breakpoint). No horizontal overflow detected in static markup.

mobile_checks.summary: All three viewports pass. Hamburger opens/closes via toggle, closes on link click (verified in nav.js source). FAQ accordion expands. Pricing tiers reflow (5-col → 2-col → 1-col). Footer reflows. No >1 H1 per page. No horizontal scroll triggers found in CSS.

performance.note: Lighthouse not run in this session — site is live but Chrome/Lighthouse runner not available in the QA environment. Expected scores based on static analysis: Performance ≥90 (vanilla HTML + 2 CSS files + 1 JS per page, no third-party scripts, no large images, OG images 53-93KB), Accessibility ≥95 (1 H1 per page, all images alt+dims, ARIA landmarks present, skip-link present, the 2 moderate color-contrast findings would land here), SEO ≥95 (canonical + sitemap + robots + JSON-LD + per-page OG + meta-description on every page, no noindex on indexable pages).

json_ld_validation.method: Local JSON.parse + Schema.org required-field spec-conformance check (Python ast). External validators (Google Rich Results, Schema.org, Bing Markup) not run in this session.

hero_headline_match.evidence: copy.json.pages.home.hero_headline = 'Cut your new-consultant ramp from twelve weeks to six.'  ↔  index.html h1.hero__headline = same string verbatim.

pricing_tier_match.evidence: All 5 tiers from pricing-strategy.json (via copy.json.pages.pricing.tiers) render with correct price_display + price_unit_label (NOT hard-coded /mo): Starter S$0 / free, up to 10 users; Team S$12 / per user / month; Agency S$8,400 / per year, up to 80 seats; Funded Programme S$6,000 / per 30-learner cohort, 12 weeks; HRMS Bundle From S$200,000 / annual ARR, 5k to 30k seat bands. Verified via parsing the live <script id='tiers-data'> JSON block.

admin_pages_data.evidence: admin.js fetches ./data/intel/competitors.json + ./data/intel/whitespace-framework.json (correct relative path — passes loadAppData() default-path pitfall from May-20 sweep). admin-insights.js fetches 3 intel files. Both pages have catch-handler logging on fetch failure. Live HEAD on /data/intel/competitors.json returns 200 OK.

anti_audience_terms.evidence: Only 2 matches and both are deliberate negative-positioning (i.e. disclaimer prose): index.html:382 'sized for white-collar office staff, not blue-collar shift workers' (intentional ICP boundary); admin.html:146,172 'frontline microlearning' as a competitor-category label for EdApp/SafetyCulture/Axonify (intentional categorisation of competitors). Source brief.constraints[] and copy.for-whom.anti-audience explicitly want this framing.  No mention of 'dormitory', 'outsourced worker', 'multilingual mobile', or 'SMS-OTP' on any rendered page.

anti_tone_terms.evidence: Zero matches for transform / unlock / empower / synergize / seamless / revolutionize / game-change in any production HTML page. Only matches were in colors.html (the internal/transient palette tryout, not in nav, robots-disallowed).

emoji_check.evidence: Python Unicode-block scan (U+1F300-U+1FAFF, U+2700-U+27BF, U+1F600-U+1F64F) returns 0 emoji on every production HTML page.

exclamation_check.evidence: Zero exclamation marks in product copy across all 9 pages (the only ! is in CSS !important inside <style> blocks and <!DOCTYPE> / <!-- HTML comments -->).

live_url_probe.note: Live deploy is current; local working tree diffs zero against live for pricing.html.

ship_blockers.impact: Every contact-form submission will POST to a non-existent Formspree endpoint and 404. Primary lead-capture pathway is broken.
ship_blockers.fix: Replace PLACEHOLDER_FORM_ID with Derrick's real Formspree form ID (provisioned for derrick@elitez.asia delivery, per brief.json.contact_form_handler='formspree').

ship_blockers.impact: On GitHub Pages (site at /elitez-lms-site/), the bare-absolute href '/contact.html' resolves to derrick-pixel.github.io/contact.html — a 404. All 5 tier-card CTAs (Start a 10-seat trial, Talk to us about Team, Request an Agency quote, Scope a funded cohort, Open a partner conversation) are broken.
ship_blockers.fix: Change href to 'contact.html#contact-form' (relative — pricing.html sits at the same path level as contact.html). Same fix would work for the canonical custom-domain (lms.elitez.com.sg) since both pages live at root there too.

warnings_non_blocking.evidence: <h4 class="site-footer__col-heading"> directly follows main's last <h2> with no <h3> between (e.g., index.html:598,607,616).
warnings_non_blocking.fix:
Either: (a) demote footer column headings from h4 to h3, (b) wrap footer in a

Site footer

... with h3 columns, or (c) add a visually-hidden

Site links

at the top of
before the column headings.
warnings_non_blocking.lesson: Footer is a landmark but its internal headings still participate in the page outline. Crawlers and screen-readers flag the gap.

warnings_non_blocking.evidence: Contrast(--muted2 #6b7785, --bg #fafafa) = 4.37; AA body requires 4.5. Contrast(--accent2 #F26522, --bg #fafafa) = 3.02; AA large-text requires 3.0, AA body 4.5.
warnings_non_blocking.fix: Darken --muted2 to #6a7480 (or use --muted #475569 for small UI strings); reserve --accent2 #F26522 for icons / borders / large headings only. Pitfall reference: palette-bad-contrast.
Change to <html lang="en-SG"> for SG-targeted SEO + correct screen-reader voice. Pitfall reference: seo-no-lang-attr (extended variant — present-but-non-region-specific).
Conventionally accepted for 404 pages. Could add a short FAQ pull or top-3-page recommendation list. Not a blocker.

warnings_non_blocking.evidence: Neither admin page has the mandatory 'Page last edited' bottom-fixed stamp per Derrick's /admin-page-stamp skill convention.
warnings_non_blocking.fix: Apply the admin-page-stamp pattern to both admin pages (date stamp at bottom-fixed position). The skill auto-triggers when editing admin/ paths — apply on next admin-page edit.

warnings_non_blocking.evidence: FAQ accordion HTML on each page is hand-written, while the FAQPage JSON-LD on index is hand-emitted from copy.json.faq[]. Two sources of truth; if Derrick edits one, the other drifts.
warnings_non_blocking.fix: Drive both renders from a single inline JSON block (similar to pricing.js + tiers-data pattern) or generate at build time. Pitfall reference: copy-hardcoded-in-html (variant).

warnings_non_blocking.evidence: colors.html exists in repo and is robots-disallowed but is NOT declared in sitemap.json.pages[] with internal:true. Agent 7 had to discover it from the directory listing.
warnings_non_blocking.fix: Add colors.html as an explicit page with 'internal': true in sitemap.json (per FIELD-DICTIONARY.md sitemap.json field notes), or remove the file from the repo since the palette has been chosen ('chosen': 'elitez-institutional-navy').

pitfall_hits.evidence: Footer h4 follows main h2 with no intermediate h3 on every page.

pitfall_proposals.story: elitez-lms-site Agent 5 (or Agent 4) emitted contact.html with action='https://formspree.io/f/PLACEHOLDER_FORM_ID' as a scaffold placeholder. Agent 6 (SEO) didn't audit form actions. Agent 7 caught it in QA — every submission would 404. Same gotcha-shape as 'robots-disallow-everything' (a placeholder slipping into production).
pitfall_proposals.fix: Agent 6's deliverable checklist should grep production HTML for the literal token 'PLACEHOLDER' (case-insensitive) across all attribute values and content; any hit fails the shipping gate. Cross-referenced against brief.json.contact_form_handler — if 'formspree', the form action must match the regex ^https://formspree\.io/f/[a-zA-Z0-9]{8,}$ (8+ char form ID).
pitfall_proposals.lesson: Placeholders in shipped HTML are a class of bug. Audit by string, not by intent.

pitfall_proposals.story: elitez-lms-site assets/js/pricing.js renders tier-card CTAs with href='/contact.html#contact-form'. On GitHub Pages where the site lives at /elitez-lms-site/, the bare-absolute path resolves to a different (non-existent) sub-path. All 5 tier-card CTAs broken. The HTML hand-written hrefs in the same project use 'contact.html' (relative) — only the JS-rendered ones got it wrong because the dev defaulted to root-absolute when reasoning in isolation about a JS template literal.
pitfall_proposals.fix: JS that emits hrefs must mirror the relative-path convention of the surrounding HTML, OR derive the base URL at runtime: const BASE = document.querySelector('link[rel="canonical"]').href.replace(/[^/]+$/, ''). Agent 7 to grep assets/js/**/*.js for href= patterns that start with '/' and aren't elitez-lms-site/-prefixed; cross-check against the project's deploy path. Pairs with seo-no-canonical pitfall.
pitfall_proposals.lesson: GitHub Pages sub-path deploys + bare-absolute hrefs = silent breakage. Either use canonical-derived base, repo-prefixed absolutes, or pure relatives — pick one and enforce.

pitfall_proposals.story: elitez-lms-site shipped with colors.html in the repo (the palette tryout, with transitions and 'transform'/'seamless' anti-tone words). It was correctly robots-disallowed but was NOT declared in sitemap.json.pages[] with internal:true. Agent 7 had to learn it was internal by inspection. Per FIELD-DICTIONARY v3.0.1, internal:true is the canonical signal — but neither Agent 2 (who emits colors.html) nor Agent 3 (who owns sitemap.json) recorded the flag.
pitfall_proposals.fix: Agent 3's sitemap.json output must list EVERY HTML file in the repo, with internal:true on files Agent 2/Agent 4 emit as transient. Agent 7's deliverable checklist gets: 'For each *.html on disk that is not in sitemap.json.pages[].path or .admin_pages[].path, log as orphan-page candidate and either flag for inclusion or for deletion.'
pitfall_proposals.lesson: If a file is in the deploy, it has to be in the sitemap — either as a real page or as internal:true. 'On disk but not in sitemap' is the silent leak path.

pitfall_proposals.story:
elitez-lms-site shipped with

Product

Company

Reach us

… on every page. Every page's main ends at h2 or h3. The h2→h4 jump is a semantic-html-audit violation on 9 pages. Same convention exists in other Elitez sites (dt-public, elitezsecurity) — likely lifted from a shared scaffold.
pitfall_proposals.source_project: elitez-lms-site (plus likely cross-project — to verify on next QA pass)
pitfall_proposals.fix: Agent 4's component library `site-footer` template should emit h3 column headings, OR wrap the footer in a sr-only h2 banner ('Site footer') so the h3→h4 jump becomes h2→h3→h4. Update the static-informational archetype CLAUDE.md `Footer` template literal. Agent 7 grep: `<h4` immediately after `</main>` is a heuristic trigger.
pitfall_proposals.lesson: Heading structure is page-wide, not section-wide. Footer columns participate in the outline.
